content.problemStatement:
Chef hosts a party for his birthday. There are NNN people at the party. All these NNN people decide to play Jenga.

There are XXX Jenga tiles available. In one round, all the players pick 111 tile each and place it in the tower.

The game is valid if:


All the players have a tile in each round;
All the tiles are used at the end.
Given NNN and XXX, find whether the game is valid.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and XXX representing the number of people at the party and the number of available tiles respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line YES\texttt{YES}YES if the game is valid, else output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and X, the number of people and the number of Jenga tiles.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the game can be played validly, otherwise "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 3 tiles, 3 people → 3 % 3 = 0 → YES.\nTest case 2: 2 tiles, 4 people → 2 % 4 != 0 → NO.\nTest case 3: 4 tiles, 2 people → 4 % 2 = 0 → YES.

solution.approach: In each round exactly N tiles are used (one per player). The game finishes exactly when all tiles are consumed, which is possible only if X is a multiple of N. Therefore for each test case we just check whether X % N == 0. If true, print YES, else NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – a constant amount of work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); X = int(next(it))
        out.append('YES' if X % N == 0 else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
